reasoning: In 2017, only 39.7% of Nigerians had a bank account. According to the 2021 World Bank Global Findex Database, account ownership in Nigeria was approximately 45%. Reaching 95% by 2024 would require an unprecedented expansion of over 50 percentage points in just three years, which is historically unprecedented without massive, state-mandated universal account drives that have completely captured the deepest rural and unbanked populations. Recent data from EFInA (2023) puts formal financial inclusion well below 95%. Based on current trends, there is virtually no chance of hitting the 95% target by Jan 1, 2025. Therefore, the probability is capped at the minimum allowed forecasting boundary.
